connectionPoolSize.description: Choose the maximum number of connections in the connection pool. Defaults to 50. Takes precedence over environment variable 'GF_PLUGINS_ORACLE_DATASOURCE_POOLSIZE' if set.

dataProxyTimeout.description: Choose the maximum time in seconds to wait for a response. Defaults to 120 seconds. Takes precedence over environment variable 'GF_DATAPROXY_TIMEOUT' if set.

prefetchRowsCount.description: Row Prefetching allow you to set the number of rows to prefetch into the client while a result set is being populated during a query. This feature reduces the number of round trips to the server.

rowLimit.description: Maximum number of rows returned per query. Defaults to 1,000,000.

timezone_name.description: Choose the default timezone of the Oracle server. Typically this is UTC.

tnsNamesEntry.description: Use connection config specified in tnsnames.ora

useKerberosAuthentication.description: Authentication discriminator: false selects Basic Authentication (username + password), true selects Kerberos Authentication. The config editor only exposes Kerberos when useTNSNamesBasedConnection is true, though the backend also accepts Kerberos with a host + port connection.

useTNSNamesBasedConnection.description: Connection-method discriminator: false selects 'Host with TCP Port' (host + port + database), true selects 'TNSNames Entry' (a tnsnames.ora entry). Written by the config editor's Connection methods selector.

use_dst.description: Parsed by the backend into DBConnectionOptions.DSTEnabled (pkg/models/settings.go:23) but never read when building the connection string. Declared in the frontend OracleOptions type (src/types.ts:9) but not written by the config editor — use_dst is a per-query/annotation option (src/types.ts:30, src/datasource.ts:109), not a datasource-level setting.

user.description: An Oracle username with access to the specified database.

url.description: Hostname or IP Address with TCP port number.

secureValues.description: An Oracle password with access to the specified database.
